scope_boundary: Local repository command-replay and artifact-digest drift detection only. The probe replays Resource Economics validators and records hashes; it is not a live workload, production scheduler log, external review, or runtime budget-enforcement result.
transition_reason: The live probe makes the Resource evidence lane replayable and drift-checkable, but its command surface is the local repository validator set. That is useful reproducibility evidence and insufficient support for live workload quality, scheduler behavior, model quality, runtime budget enforcement, or economic outcomes.
required_artifacts: production or externally reviewable scheduler logs
negative_results: tracked artifact digests must match current files
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: tracked artifact digest drift without regenerated result
local replay described as live workload review
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed replay claim after public-safe live or external workload traces, scheduler logs, runtime budget records, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent workload, scheduler, economics, or source-interpretation audit
non_claims: does not promote Resource Economics above argument
does not prove live workload quality
does not prove deployed scheduler or runtime budget-enforcement behavior
does not create an upward support-state transition
